title: Luffy Lands in Wano and Meets Tama
description: On pages 11–14, this part focuses on Unknown person #9, Unknown person #10, Luffy, Unknown person #11, Zoro, Unknown person #12 as Luffy lands in Wano, meets Tama, and immediately collides with the poverty and cruelty of Kaido’s rule.
scene: Luffy washes up in Wano, meets Tama, and discovers a country suffering under Kaido’s rule.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Yes!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: That's it!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Drop your hips! Your hand is too low!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: You're crazy! Keep it together!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: HAA... HAA...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: At this rate, there's no way

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Shogun will summon you from his chambers

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: In such a short time!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The first time I was in the middle of the room was that I was

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Listen here, friends...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This is the Wano Country. The Shogun's name is Kurozumi Crochi.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: His officials and underlings stink of Kaido. Every last one.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's why they're being allowed to run amok, but... if we lay a hand on them,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Kaido will get wind of it! Fast!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Until our comrades arrive, and we've made the necessary preparations for our fight, we cannot allow ourselves to be found out!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: First, we will become citizens of this land. Quietly, calmly...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Carry out this mission! Please!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It seems the magistrate's finally caught the killer.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And that Killer's going to commit seppuki now!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm so glad! There've been three victims this month alone!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: It's horrible! My husband was killed, too!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: We knew thee as a killer, and...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Peace will return to our streets with thy death.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But, to think thou were a grave robber, too!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Amidst the confusion of the pirate incident, two things did disappear!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: 23 years ago...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The body of the legendary master swordsman, Ryluma,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: And his famed sword, Shusui!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Truth be told, though, killer...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Thou doth not look terribly old.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Didst thou Purloin this from some petty bandit?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Fufu...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It matters not. I shall see for myself the deadly beauty of this edge soon!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: For that famed blade, Shusui, Doth now rest in my hands!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: this knife. It doesn't have a hilt... um, eh. Hilteth.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: seppuku Daggers are short, and do not normally have guards.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Thou shalt not be fighting with it, anyway. It should suffice.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Dost thou even know the proper method of committing seppuku?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If thou hast even the barest shred of honor in thy veins, admit thy sins,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: And lead thyself from this world!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: To save thee unneeded misery, I shall behead thee in mercy soon after.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Fine...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: FARE- WELL!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Hmph. Save us thy unseemly groveling.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The true worth of a warrior's life is found in its last minutes!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There are witnesses before there! More than ample evidence!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A honorable death, in truth unbecoming one such as thee! Rejoice in thy seppuki!

summary: This section advances the Wano Country Arrival storyline with 48 dialogue beats. Luffy’s first steps in Wano establish Tama’s kindness, the country’s hunger, and the brutality of Kaido’s forces.
